data.title: Russia Central Bank Head Says Crypto Transfers Abroad Face No Investor Restrictions
data.description: Russian Central Bank Governor Elvira Nabiullina said there are no restrictions on transferring cryptocurrency from Russia to foreign destinations for any category of investor. According to Foresight News, she added that investors should note such transactions currently lack legal protection.

data.title: Market Opening Volume and Momentum Remain Below 30-Day Averages
data.description: Data as of July 24, 2026 showed that volume in the 15 minutes before the U.S. market open was at the 17th percentile, below the 30-day average. According to Jin10, opening follow-through strength was in a relatively low range, and market continuation momentum was at the 20th percentile, also below the 30-day average, indicating overall continuation ability remained relatively weak.

data.title: Kremlin Says Russia Views Trump’s Ukraine War Efforts as Sincere
data.description:
The Kremlin said on Friday that Russia believes U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to end the war in Ukraine are sincere, while noting that the United States continues to supply weapons to Kyiv. According to Odaily, Russia will wait for Washington to present a new solution.

Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Russia will continue to pursue its stated goals by military means. He said military action is not the preferred route, but Russia will continue until it achieves a complete victory, while waiting for a new U.S. proposal.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Gilts Face Twin Risks From Burnham's Debut and Oil Shock
data.description:
Britain’s bond traders are weighing two pressures in Andy Burnham’s first week in office: his new government’s spending ambitions and oil prices hovering near $100 a barrel, according to Bloomberg.

The combination is shaping pricing in the gilt market as investors assess the fiscal outlook alongside the impact of higher energy costs.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Paraná Farmers Tokenize 10 Dairy Cows to Raise Nearly $20,000 in Credit
data.description: Farmers in Paraná, Brazil, tokenized 10 dairy cows on the B3 stock exchange and raised nearly $20,000 in credit. According to NS3.AI, Cowmed led the project and used AI-powered tracking collars to create encrypted digital identities for each cow. Cowmed said it expects up to 20% of its monitored network to adopt the model.

data.title: Lamb Weston Sees Flat Growth as Customers Trade Down
data.description:
Lamb Weston Holdings Inc. gave modest guidance for the coming fiscal year as restaurant customers switched to cheaper products and diners ate out less often, according to Bloomberg.

The potato supplier to restaurants including McDonald’s Corp. said the trend pointed to flat growth.

data.title: STOCKS | Verizon Signs $1 Billion Data Center Deal With Google
data.description: Verizon signed a $1 billion data center agreement with Google. According to Jin10, the deal was announced in the source.

data.title: Drone Strike Hits U.S.-Linked Logistics Warehouse in Saudi Arabia
data.description: A logistics warehouse in Saudi Arabia linked to the U.S. military was hit by a drone attack, with explosions reported at the scene. According to ChainCatcher, the incident was reported by Jin10.

data.title: AI Data Center Boom Fuels Intel Turnaround
data.description: Intel Corp.’s long-awaited turnaround is underway as demand from artificial intelligence data centers boosts need for its chips, according to Bloomberg. The company is benefiting from the surge in AI infrastructure spending as data centers expand their hardware purchases.

data.title: Darline Graham Faces Tough Path To Full Senate Term In South Carolina
data.description: According to Axios, Darline Graham, who has President Donald Trump's endorsement and is serving as a temporary replacement after the death of her brother, Sen. Lindsey Graham, faces an uphill battle to win a full six-year Senate term in South Carolina. The state's Aug. 11 Republican primary has become a test of whether Trump's backing, the Graham name, and her brother's political network are enough to overcome concerns from GOP operatives and early polls showing her trailing rivals Rep. Ralph Norman and Rep. Russell Fry. Republicans cited three obstacles: Lindsey Graham's mixed standing with South Carolina conservatives, Darline Graham's low profile and limited political identity, and the state's history of rejecting perceived political succession. Axios also reported that Lindsey Graham's campaign fund has more than $2 million on hand, Darline Graham's advisers are exploring whether she can legally access those funds, and a super PAC supporting the late senator plans to spend $900,000 to back her campaign.

data.title: HSBC initiates SpaceX at hold, sees shares fully valued at $115
data.description: According to CNBC, HSBC initiated coverage of SpaceX with a hold rating and a $115 price target, saying the company is already fully valued even after assigning a premium for Elon Musk's track record. The bank said its sum-of-the-parts approach includes a 2 times innovation premium and puts the target below SpaceX's $135 IPO price and recent trading level of about $118. HSBC said investors already appear to be pricing in much of SpaceX's long-term growth potential, including expansion of Starlink, higher launch activity and development of its AI initiatives. The firm also said its most optimistic blue-sky scenario values SpaceX at $293 per share if Starship becomes commercially viable in 2027, launch capacity doubles versus the base case, Starlink reaches a larger addressable market with higher average revenue per user, and the company's AI assets receive richer valuation multiples.

data.title: Wall Street Calls: UBS Upgrades Amkor, Baird Lifts Apple Target, HSBC Starts SpaceX at Hold
data.description: According to CNBC, UBS upgraded Amkor Technology to buy from neutral, saying the company's US$1.5 billion NVIDIA strategic partnership supports Amkor's ongoing ramp of Vera CPU. Baird reiterated Apple as outperform and raised its price target to $330 per share from $310. HSBC initiated SpaceX at hold, citing valuation, while Raymond James upgraded Teck Resources to outperform from market perform on expectations for accelerating growth. UBS also reiterated Advanced Micro Devices as buy and lifted its price target to $730 from $700 after the company's AI Day, and Bank of America reiterated Intel as buy following earnings, saying data center sales rose 59% year over year. Elsewhere, Morgan Stanley reiterated Nvidia as overweight on the Amkor deal, BMO upgraded Dover to outperform from market perform with a $240 target, and Piper Sandler lowered Tesla's price target to $450 from $500 after Q2 results and the 10-Q.

data.title: Poolin Technology Files for Chapter 11 With $163.7 Million in Wallet-Holder IOUs
data.description:
Poolin Technology filed for Chapter 11 with $163.7 million in unsecured wallet-holder IOUs owed to about 11,700 users. According to NS3.AI, its Texas affiliates are seeking court approval for asset sales with opening offers worth $52 million.

The offers equal 31.8% of the IOUs, but they cover Lonestar affiliate assets rather than cash in Poolin Technology's wallet business. Wallet recoveries remain uncertain because sale prices, liens, estate allocations, expenses, and allowed claims have not been resolved.

data.title: Southbound East Asia Welcomes SFC Statement On Single-Day L&I Framework
data.description:
Southbound East Asia, manager of Hong Kong's largest single-stock leveraged and inverse product, XL2 South Hynix (7709), said it welcomes the Securities and Futures Commission's latest statement on optimizing the single-day L&I regulatory framework, according to Ming Pao.

The company said it has been closely monitoring relevant regulatory developments and will carefully study the contents of the SFC circular issued today to assess the applicability of the related regulatory arrangements.

data.title: CATL Plans Up to RMB 40 Billion A-Share Buyback
data.description:
CATL (3750) plans to use no less than RMB 20 billion and no more than RMB 40 billion to repurchase A shares through centralized bidding, with a maximum buyback price of RMB 573 per share, according to Ming Pao.

The company said the ceiling is no higher than 1.5 times the average trading price of its shares over the 30 trading days before the board approved the buyback resolution.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Supply in Profit Approaches 60% — But CryptoQuant Warns This Looks Like the False Breakout That Failed in June
data.description:
Bitcoin supply in profit has climbed to 57.5% as of July 22 — up from 46.2% on June 30, the 2026 low — approaching the 60% threshold that feels like meaningful recovery progress. But CryptoQuant contributor thechessONCHAIN is issuing a specific and data-grounded warning: this cycle has already produced one failed attempt at exactly this stage, and the current setup is not yet meeting the onchain requirements that have historically confirmed bear market endings. From April 28 to June 1, LTH-SOPR's 30-day SMA held above 1.0 for 35 days, supply in profit reached 67%, and both rolled back over — producing the June 30 low of 46.2% that the current recovery is climbing away from. The 30-day SMA of LTH-SOPR has now been below 1 for more than 50 consecutive days.
The Two Confirmation Requirements — Neither Is Met
CryptoQuant's thechessONCHAIN has identified the specific conditions that have ended previous Bitcoin bear markets based on onchain data — not price action alone. Both conditions must be met simultaneously, and both must be sustained rather than briefly touched.
The first requirement is that the 30-day SMA of LTH-SOPR remains above 1.0 without falling below that level for weeks on end. LTH-SOPR measures the proportion of long-term holder coins moving onchain at a higher price relative to their previous transaction — values above 1 indicate LTH coins moving mostly in profit, values below 1 indicate LTHs moving coins at a loss in what can be interpreted as capitulatory behavior. The current 30-day SMA of LTH-SOPR has been below 1 for more than 50 consecutive days — meaning long-term holders have been consistently transacting at a loss for nearly two months, even as the nominal Bitcoin price has recovered from $57,750 to $65,000.
The second requirement is that total supply in profit exceeds 64%. Current supply in profit at 57.5% is 6.5 percentage points below that threshold — a gap that, given Bitcoin's current price trajectory, would require approximately another 10-15% price appreciation to close depending on the distribution of acquisition costs in the current supply.
The Failed Breakout Template — April 28 to June 1
The most analytically sobering element of thechessONCHAIN's analysis is the precision of the prior failed breakout. From April 28 to June 1, the LTH-SOPR 30-day SMA held above 1.0 for 35 consecutive days — a duration that in prior cycles had been associated with genuine bear market endings. Supply in profit reached 67% — above the 64% confirmation threshold. Both indicators simultaneously met the historically required levels. And both rolled back over, producing the June 30 low of 46.2% supply in profit that was below 50% — meaning more than half of all Bitcoin in existence was being held at a loss.
The failed April-June breakout is the specific reason why the current 57.5% supply in profit reading cannot be treated as straightforward recovery confirmation. The market has demonstrated in 2026 that meeting the quantitative thresholds for a short period is insufficient — the thresholds must be sustained for an extended period without rolling back over. The April-June attempt held for 35 days before failing. The current recovery has been building for approximately 22 days since the June 30 low — not yet at the duration where prior cycle confirmations have held.
LTH-SOPR Below 1 for 50+ Days — The Capitulation Signal
The LTH-SOPR 30-day SMA remaining below 1 for more than 50 consecutive days is the most important caveat in an otherwise improving onchain picture. Long-term holders — defined as entities whose Bitcoin has remained dormant for at least six months — are the most conviction-driven cohort in the Bitcoin supply. When their SOPR falls below 1 for extended periods, it indicates they are being forced or choosing to transact at prices below their acquisition cost — the behavioral signature of capitulatory selling or distribution under duress.
Fifty-plus days of LTH-SOPR below 1 occurring simultaneously with the whale accumulation data showing 270,000 BTC absorbed over two weeks and the Glassnode accumulation score at 1.0 creates an apparent contradiction. The resolution is that the LTH-SOPR measures LTH coins that are being transacted onchain — the ones moving. The accumulation score and exchange supply data measure the overall LTH supply and the coins available on exchanges. What the combined picture suggests is that a subset of long-term holders who need or choose to transact are doing so at a loss, while the majority of LTH supply — 79% of circulating supply per K33 data — is being held without transacting, being absorbed by whales rather than flowing to exchanges.
The Demand Picture — Institutional Versus Spot
Demand appears mixed, with weak spot-market interest meeting a rebound in institutional BTC allocation. The six-day $930 million ETF inflow streak reflects institutional demand returning through the regulated channel — the most sustained institutional buying since April. But the subdued spot market volume that has characterized every recovery session this week — prices rising on fewer sellers rather than more aggressive buyers — confirms the weak spot-market interest that CoinTelegraph flagged. Institutional ETF demand and whale on-chain accumulation are providing the structural bid. Retail spot demand has not returned in a volume-confirmed way. Bear market endings historically require retail spot demand to join institutional and LTH accumulation in the final leg — the current configuration has two of three.
The 64% Threshold — What Bitcoin Price Would Close the Gap
Supply in profit at 57.5% versus the 64% confirmation requirement means approximately 6.5% more of the total Bitcoin supply needs to move into profit — which requires the Bitcoin price to appreciate sufficiently that coins acquired at prices between the current 57.5th and 64th percentile of cost basis become profitable. Given the distribution of Bitcoin's supply by acquisition price in 2026, closing that gap likely requires Bitcoin to sustain somewhere in the $68,000-$72,000 range for an extended period — which is precisely the target zone of the $5 billion Deribit options cluster at $70,000-$72,000. The options market's bullish concentration and the CryptoQuant confirmation framework are pointing at the same price zone for different reasons.
The critical word is sustain. The April-June false breakout briefly touched 67% supply in profit before rolling back. Touching $70,000-$72,000 on a single candle or during a FOMC-reaction rally is insufficient. The supply in profit metric needs to hold above 64% for weeks on end while LTH-SOPR's 30-day SMA simultaneously recovers above 1.0 — a duration requirement that cannot be satisfied by the July 31 options expiry window.
The Honest Assessment — Recovery Real, Bear Market Ending Unconfirmed
The most accurate framing of Bitcoin's current onchain position is that the recovery from the June 30 low is real, structurally supported, and better than the April-June failed breakout in some dimensions — but not yet meeting the quantitative thresholds that have historically confirmed bear market endings. Supply in profit has improved more rapidly than the April-June recovery's early stages. Whale accumulation and exchange supply tightening are more extreme than at the April-June period. ETF demand is returning. But LTH-SOPR remains below 1 after 50+ days, supply in profit at 57.5% is 6.5 percentage points below the 64% threshold, and the cycle has already demonstrated that meeting these metrics briefly is insufficient — they must be sustained.
The FOMC meeting July 28-29 and oil above $97 heading into that meeting are the immediate macro variables that will determine whether supply in profit continues climbing toward 64% or rolls back over for the second time in 2026.

data.title: Momentum Crash Hits YOLO Traders’ Returns by Most in Four Years
data.description:
A rapid selloff in the high-flying momentum trade is hurting retail traders, the strategy’s staunchest bulls, according to Bloomberg.

The move is damaging returns in a way not seen in four years, underscoring how quickly sentiment has reversed in one of the market’s most popular trades.

data.title: Russia's Central Bank Governor Nabiullina Says Bank Discussed Rate Hike and Other Scenarios
data.description: According to Jin10, Russia's central bank Governor Nabiullina said the bank discussed a rate hike and other scenarios today, and also discussed the option of keeping rates unchanged.

data.title: Whale Wallet Deposits 8,000 ETH to CEX After 8 Months
data.description: BlockBeats reported on July 24 that a whale wallet deposited 8,000 ETH into a centralized exchange (CEX) after holding the assets for eight months. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the wallet moved the ETH to the exchange after the long holding period.

data.title: Petrobras P-52 Platform Remains Shut After Gas Leak
data.description:
Brazilian state-controlled oil producer Petrobras said its P-52 offshore production platform has remained shut for the past nine months after a flammable gas leak and still has no timetable to restart, according to Bloomberg.

The outage underscores continuing operational disruption at the platform, though the company gave no further details.
